behaviors.description: Content passed to summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker, deployment log).
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at the data aggregation layer. A Unit test of individual fetchers wouldn't catch if one source is omitted during aggregation. Integration testing with mocked API clients lets you verify all three sources are collected and present in the aggregated data structure passed to summary generation—catching aggregation bugs that Unit tests would miss.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three API clients with test data. Call the aggregation/data-collection function. Verify the returned data dict contains keys for all three sources (metrics, issues, logs) with correct content. Verify no source is missing, truncated, or corrupted during aggregation.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at the error-handling and aggregation layer. A Unit test of isolated error handling wouldn't verify the end-to-end behavior: that the exception is caught, aggregation continues with partial data, and the note appears in the output. Integration testing with one mocked API client raising an exception catches the real failure mode: silent failures (note missing) or unhandled exceptions that crash the script.
behaviors.test_description: Mock one API client to raise a timeout or connection exception. Call the full report pipeline. Verify: (1) no unhandled exception is raised, (2) the aggregated data includes a 'data unavailable' note for that source, (3) the note appears in the final report output, (4) summary generation proceeds with partial data.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the LLM generation itself. Summary accuracy depends on what a real LLM produces—hallucinations (invented metrics), omissions, or misinterpretations are possible and vary non-deterministically across runs. Mocking the LLM at Integration level only verifies data is passed correctly; it misses real failures like 'the LLM invented a metric not in the input' or 'the LLM got the deployment count wrong.' You must call a real LLM to catch these failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create test data with specific, verifiable key values (e.g., '3 critical issues', 'deployment took 45 minutes', 'CPU 78%'). Call the real LLM with the data in the expected prompt format. Parse the generated summary. Verify each key value is mentioned and accurate, with no hallucinated metrics. Run multiple times to detect non-determinism.

behaviors.description: A complete report—from data collection through email delivery—reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at the email service layer, which depends on real SMTP/API behavior, network connectivity, and email client handling. A mocked email client at Integration level only verifies the recipient and content are passed correctly; it doesn't catch real failures like SMTP authentication errors, encoding issues, spam filtering, or timeout errors. You must call a real email service to verify actual delivery to an inbox.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a test email service (Mailhog, AWS SES test endpoint, etc.) with a known recipient address. Run the full script: collect real (or mocked) data, generate the summary with a real LLM, format and send the email. Verify the email is received at the inbox. Check headers, content encoding, and that the report body is intact and readable.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at the fallback/retry logic layer. A Unit test of isolated error handling or file I/O wouldn't verify the integrated behavior: that the exception is caught, the report is saved with correct content, and the next run detects and reattempts delivery. Integration testing with a mocked email service that fails, combined with real filesystem operations, catches the real failure modes: file not saved, file corrupted, or retry logic not triggered on next run.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the email service to raise an exception. Call the report script. Verify: (1) no unhandled exception, (2) a file is written to the fallback directory with the report content, (3) the file is readable and valid. Simulate the next scheduled run by calling the retry-check function. Verify it detects the saved file and reattempts email delivery. Optionally verify the file is cleaned up after successful retry.
